description: Lightweight npm CLI that syncs a single set of skills/rules/suggestions to Cursor (.mdc files) and Claude Code (CLAUDE.md) from a unified Markdown source format.

userStories.title: Project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full TypeScript project structure in place so all subsequent stories have a working build and test environment to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json created with name 'agent-skills', version '0.1.0', type 'module', license 'MIT'
Dependencies include: gray-matter, js-yaml, commander
DevDependencies include: typescript, jest, ts-jest, @types/jest, eslint, prettier, @types/node, @types/js-yaml
jest.config.ts created using ts-jest preset: { preset: 'ts-jest', testEnvironment: 'node' }
tsconfig.json configured with strict: true, target: ES2022, module: NodeNext, outDir: dist
eslint.config.js configured for TypeScript
.prettierrc created with sensible defaults
src/ directory created with empty cli.ts, index.ts, types.ts, hasher.ts, lockfile.ts
src/commands/ directory created with empty init.ts, sync.ts, check.ts, validate.ts, help.ts
src/adapters/ directory created with empty cursor.ts, claude.ts
tests/ directory created with empty cursor.test.ts, claude.test.ts, lockfile.test.ts, validate.test.ts
npm run build (tsc) exits with code 0
npm run test (jest) exits with code 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared types and hasher utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared TypeScript types and a SHA-256 file hasher so all other modules have a common contract and hashing primitive.
acceptanceCriteria: types.ts exports: SourceFile { path, type, name, description, targets, content }, LockEntry { sourcePath, sourceHash, outputPath, outputHash, timestamp }, Lockfile { entries: LockEntry[] }, TargetValue ('cursor' | 'claude' | 'all'), TypeValue ('skill' | 'rule' | 'suggestion')
hasher.ts exports hashFile(filePath: string): Promise<string> using Node crypto SHA-256
hasher.ts exports hashString(content: string): string for in-memory hashing
Tests in validate.test.ts cover hashString producing consistent output using Jest (describe/it/expect)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Init command — directory and config setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `agent-skills init` so that my ~/.agent-skills/ directory is created with the correct structure and a starter config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/init.ts implements runInit(options: { guide?: boolean, force?: boolean }): Promise<void>
Creates ~/.agent-skills/ if it does not exist
Creates ~/.agent-skills/skills/, rules/, suggestions/ subdirectories
Creates ~/.agent-skills/config.json with { version: 1, targets: ['cursor', 'claude'] } if not present; does not overwrite without --force
Prints a summary of what was created (e.g. '✓ Created ~/.agent-skills/skills/')
Function throws with a descriptive message on filesystem errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Init --guide flag — generate GUIDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `agent-skills init --guide` to write a GUIDE.md so I have a reference for the source file format and each agent's native format.
acceptanceCriteria: When options.guide is true, runInit writes ~/.agent-skills/GUIDE.md
GUIDE.md contains a section explaining skills vs rules vs suggestions with one example each
GUIDE.md contains a section documenting the source file format: Markdown with YAML frontmatter fields (type, name, description, targets)
GUIDE.md contains a section on Cursor .mdc output format (description and alwaysApply frontmatter fields)
GUIDE.md contains a section on Claude Code CLAUDE.md output format (agent-skills:start/end HTML comment delimiters)
GUIDE.md contains a worked example source file and its two generated outputs
Does not overwrite existing GUIDE.md unless --force is passed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `agent-skills validate` to check my source files for format errors so I get fast feedback before syncing.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/validate.ts exports runValidate(): Promise<{ valid: boolean; errors: ValidationError[] }>
ValidationError has shape: { file: string; line?: number; message: string }
Reads all .md files from ~/.agent-skills/skills/, rules/, suggestions/ using gray-matter
Reports error for files with unknown frontmatter keys (keys outside: type, name, description, targets)
Reports error for files with invalid type value (not 'skill' | 'rule' | 'suggestion')
Reports error for files with invalid targets value (not 'cursor' | 'claude' | 'all')
Reports warning (not error) for files missing targets field — does not block sync
Reports error for files with empty body (no content beyond frontmatter)
Returns { valid: true, errors: [] } when all files pass
Tests in validate.test.ts cover: valid file, unknown key, invalid type, invalid targets, empty body, missing targets warning
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Cursor adapter — generate .mdc files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Cursor adapter to translate source files into valid .mdc files so Cursor picks them up under .cursor/rules/.
acceptanceCriteria: adapters/cursor.ts exports writeCursorRules(sources: SourceFile[], outDir: string): Promise<string[]> returning list of written file paths
Skips SourceFiles where targets is not 'cursor' or 'all'
Output filename is kebab-case of the source filename (without extension), e.g. my-skill.md → my-skill.mdc
Output file starts with YAML frontmatter block containing description (from source frontmatter or empty string) and alwaysApply: true
YAML frontmatter is serialized using js-yaml (not hand-rolled strings)
Output file body is the source Markdown content (unchanged)
Creates outDir if it does not exist
Overwrites existing .mdc files
Tests in cursor.test.ts cover: cursor target, all target, non-cursor target skipped, kebab-case filename, frontmatter correctness
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Claude adapter — merge into CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Claude adapter to merge generated sections into CLAUDE.md without destroying manually written content.
acceptanceCriteria: adapters/claude.ts exports writeClaudeMd(sources: SourceFile[], claudeMdPath: string): Promise<void>
Skips SourceFiles where targets is not 'claude' or 'all'
Each source file is written as a section delimited by <!-- agent-skills:start:<slug> --> and <!-- agent-skills:end:<slug> --> where slug is the kebab-case filename
Re-running replaces only the delimited sections; all content outside delimiters is preserved verbatim
If CLAUDE.md does not exist, creates it with only the generated sections
Generated section header inside the delimiters is a level-2 heading using source name or filename
Tests in claude.test.ts cover: new file creation, section replacement on re-run, manual content preserved, multiple sources, non-claude target skipped
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Lockfile module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lockfile module that reads and writes agent-skills.lock so sync and check have a shared persistence layer.
acceptanceCriteria: lockfile.ts exports readLockfile(path: string): Promise<Lockfile | null> — returns null if file does not exist
lockfile.ts exports writeLockfile(path: string, data: Lockfile): Promise<void>
Lockfile JSON is pretty-printed with JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)
Keys within each LockEntry are written in stable order: sourcePath, sourceHash, outputPath, outputHash, timestamp
timestamp is ISO 8601 string (new Date().toISOString())
Tests in lockfile.test.ts cover: write then read round-trip, null on missing file, stable key order
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Sync command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `agent-skills sync` to validate, translate, and write all output files plus update agent-skills.lock in the current working directory.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/sync.ts exports runSync(cwd: string): Promise<void>
Calls runValidate() first; if errors exist, prints each error and throws without writing any output files
Reads all source files from ~/.agent-skills/skills/, rules/, suggestions/ using gray-matter
Calls writeCursorRules() with outDir = path.join(cwd, '.cursor/rules')
Calls writeClaudeMd() with claudeMdPath = path.join(cwd, 'CLAUDE.md')
For each input→output pair, hashes source file and output file using hashFile()
Writes agent-skills.lock to path.join(cwd, 'agent-skills.lock') with all LockEntry records
Prints a summary: count of .mdc files written, whether CLAUDE.md was updated, lockfile path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Check command — CI enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `agent-skills check` to compare current file hashes against agent-skills.lock so CI fails when output files are out of sync.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/check.ts exports runCheck(cwd: string): Promise<{ inSync: boolean; diffs: SyncDiff[] }>
SyncDiff has shape: { file: string; reason: 'source-changed' | 'output-missing' | 'output-modified' }
If agent-skills.lock does not exist: prints "No agent-skills.lock found. Run `agent-skills sync` first." and returns inSync: false
For each LockEntry: re-hashes sourcePath and outputPath using hashFile()
Reports 'source-changed' if current source hash !== stored sourceHash
Reports 'output-missing' if output file does not exist
Reports 'output-modified' if current output hash !== stored outputHash
If inSync: prints "All files are in sync."
If not inSync: prints each diff with filename and reason
CLI exits with code 0 when inSync, code 1 when not
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Help command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `agent-skills help` to print command documentation to stdout so I can quickly reference usage without reading docs.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/help.ts exports runHelp(command?: string): void
With no argument, prints usage summary for all commands: init, sync, check, validate, help
Each command entry shows: name, one-line description, flags/options, example invocation
runHelp('init') prints detailed help for init including --guide and --force flags
runHelp('sync') prints detailed help for sync
runHelp('check') prints detailed help for check with CI usage example
runHelp('validate') prints detailed help for validate
runHelp('help') prints help for help
Unknown command name prints error and lists valid commands
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI entrypoint and npm package wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete CLI entrypoint so the tool works as `agent-skills <cmd>` whether installed globally, as a dev dependency, or via npx.
acceptanceCriteria: cli.ts uses commander to register subcommands: init, sync, check, validate, help
cli.ts has shebang line #!/usr/bin/env node as first line of compiled output (add to src via tsconfig or package.json build step)
init command wires --guide and --force flags to runInit()
sync command calls runSync(process.cwd())
check command calls runCheck(process.cwd()) and calls process.exit(1) if not inSync
validate command calls runValidate() and calls process.exit(1) if not valid
help command calls runHelp() or runHelp(subcommand)
package.json bin field: { "agent-skills": "./dist/cli.js" }
package.json main field: "./dist/index.js"
package.json scripts include: build, test (jest), lint, format, prepublishOnly: "npm run build"
index.ts re-exports runSync, runCheck, runValidate, runInit for programmatic use
npm run build produces dist/cli.js with executable permissions
Typecheck passes
